activeHolds.update: Claude Opus 4.7 released May 13. Claude for Small Business launched. Mythos Preview cybersecurity capabilities confirmed. White House carve-out drafting ongoing. Pentagon blacklist maintained. Court injunction pausing Trump directive allowing continued federal agency use. Hold expires tomorrow — full assessment queued.

Closing arguments in Musk v. Altman set for May 14–15 (testimony wrapped May 13). Nadella testified May 11: Musk never raised concerns to him; Microsoft to spend $100B+ on OpenAI by June 2026. Hold expires tomorrow — assess post-closing-argument regardless of verdict.

activeHolds.status: HOLD EXPIRES TODAY — release for tomorrow cycle

EU DMA comment period closed May 13. EU Commission team dispatched to Budapest (Hungary context). No new material Google-specific compassion signal today beyond ongoing antitrust regime. Queue for May 15 standard rotation assessment.
activeHolds.note: Per active hold instruction: if material news today, flag for May 15 cycle. No acute compassion event found today — queue as standard rotation.

activeHolds.update: Closing arguments beginning May 14–15. Advisory jury to deliberate. Judge Gonzalez Rogers expected ruling by mid-May. xAI admission: distills OpenAI models (Musk v. Altman week 1 testimony). Trial ongoing — hold maintained through verdict.

May 10-11 casualty baseline: 4 killed, 16 injured (KPK). No mass-casualty event found in 24h window as of scan time — active weather system continues through May 17. NDMA active. Forward signal remains live.

EU Commission team dispatched to Budapest (Euronews, May 13) to assess 10.4B EUR fund release pathway. Magyar targeting end-of-May rule of law reform deadline. Brussels visit pencilled ~May 25. No concrete legislative action yet — intent phase continuing. Re-assess June 12 per schedule unless legislative milestone triggers earlier.

pendingUnappliedChanges: Hungary band crossing (37.5 → 41.4, Developing → Functional) — pending

prioritizedEntities.name: xAI
prioritizedEntities.trigger: On May 14 at 3:15 AM PST, an unauthorized modification was made to Grok's system prompt on X directing the chatbot to generate politically biased responses; xAI disclosed the incident publicly, stated the code review process was circumvented by an employee, and announced Grok system prompts will be published openly on GitHub going forward.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: floor — first baseline, compound harm cluster
prioritizedEntities.notes: This is a same-day event (May 14 itself). The prompt manipulation incident is the third distinct Grok safety/governance failure category: (1) CSAM/NCII generation at scale Jan 2026, (2) Musk-protection prompt manipulation Feb 2026, (3) political-bias unauthorized prompt modification May 14 2026. Each incident compounds the governance signal. The public disclosure and GitHub transparency pledge is a weak positive INT signal but does not offset the pattern. xAI has not been assessed — this is now the most urgently unassessed entity in the AI-labs index. The May 15 model retirement notice (docs.x.ai) adds a separate dimension: Grok model lifecycle transparency. Assess this cycle with all three incident categories in scope.

prioritizedEntities.name: Myanmar
prioritizedEntities.trigger: CDM May 14 domestic news reports: Bilin Township (Mon State) — 2 killed including a child, 6 injured in air and ground attacks on Wintarpan village; Mindat (Chin State) — 11 airstrikes within 2 days, 4 killed including detainees; ongoing airstrike pattern across Tanintharyi, Mon, Sagaing. IIMM warned children face ongoing serious risks, citing one-year anniversary of Depayin school attack (20+ students killed).
prioritizedEntities.notes: Floor entity. Three discrete airstrike events today (May 14) plus May 6-12 cluster previously documented. Assess as multi-event conduct accumulation: Bilin township (civilian vehicle + village), Mindat (11 airstrikes in 2 days, detainee deaths), continuing Sagaing/Karen/Chin pattern. IIMM school anniversary statement is a new accountability-dimension signal. Min Aung Hlaing '100-day peace plan' (announced April 21) is demonstrably contradicted by ongoing airstrike conduct — this is an ACC gap event. No score change from floor, but documentation required.

prioritizedEntities.name: Russia
prioritizedEntities.trigger: May 14 large-scale missile attack across Ukraine: 1 killed, 36 injured in Kyiv including 11 rescued from collapsed building; 1,560+ drones deployed over 24 hours, 670 attack drones and 56 missiles in single overnight attack; residential buildings struck.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: floor — conduct accumulation, post-ceasefire offensive surge documentation
prioritizedEntities.notes: Floor entity. Last assessed April 19 — 25 days of conduct accumulation to document. The May 14 attack (1,560+ drones, 56 missiles, 1 killed, 36 injured) represents the largest single-day documented strike in this assessment window. Combine with: post-ceasefire offensive surge (150+ engagements during declared ceasefire May 9-11), resumption of mass offensive May 12. This is a 'continued conduct while ceasefire announced' category event — assess the ceasefire conduct window as a distinct analytical category. No score change from floor.

prioritizedEntities.name: Sudan
prioritizedEntities.trigger: Drone strikes May 12 hit Kornoi (North Darfur) market, civilian vehicles, and water well killing civilians; May 8 strikes killed 26 in South Kordofan and North Kordofan; UN documents 880+ civilians killed by drones in first 4 months of 2026; drone warfare spreading beyond Kordofan/Darfur to Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum; airport strike May 4 disrupted all flights.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: floor — new conduct categories: drone warfare expansion and civilian infrastructure targeting
prioritizedEntities.notes: Floor entity. Two new conduct categories since last assessment (May 6): (1) geographic spread of drone warfare to Blue Nile, White Nile, Khartoum — conflict is no longer regionalized; (2) airport strike (May 4, Khartoum International) — civilian infrastructure disruption at national scale. Combined with May 12 Kornoi water well strike — systematic destruction of survival infrastructure. UN OHCHR 'high alert' designation (May 14 period) is a new INT-dimension accountability signal. Banjul Joint Declaration remains pending documentation.

prioritizedEntities.name: Israel
prioritizedEntities.trigger: Ceasefire under acute strain: May 3 Israel threatened to resume war to force Hamas disarmament; aid trucks averaging below agreed 600/day threshold; 800+ killed since October ceasefire; 8 aid workers killed; Gaza humanitarian situation described as 'far from over' by OCHA; negotiations stalled.
prioritizedEntities.notes: Floor entity. Last assessed May 5. The May 3 war-resumption threat (to force disarmament) is a new coercive-diplomacy signal distinct from prior ceasefire-violation documentation. The 6-month OCHA review is a new primary-source document covering the full ceasefire period: famine averted, 1.1M people reached monthly — these are limited positive signals that must be recorded. However, 800+ killed during ceasefire and 8 aid workers killed are continued negative conduct signals. The dual positive/negative evidence requires careful dimensional allocation. Hungary ICC reversal (new government, enforce warrants) is a pending INT signal to document.

prioritizedEntities.name: India
prioritizedEntities.trigger: Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill signed into law March 2026 (Amnesty: 'major step backward'); HRW April 2026 online censorship expansion rules proposed; May 9 arrest for Instagram post criticizing Operation Sindoor; Fortify Rights April 9 call on Thailand to protect 36 Rohingya refugees forced into sea by India in May 2025 still unresolved — no accountability.
prioritizedEntities.notes: Last assessed May 6. Since then: (1) Transgender Amendment Bill signed into law — removes self-identification right affirmed by Supreme Court 2014, allows medical data sharing with authorities. This is a measurable EQU regression. (2) April 17 HRW online censorship expansion — proposed rules targeting dissent; (3) May 9 Nagpur arrest for Instagram post (Operation Sindoor criticism) — SYS enforcement against speech. (4) Fortify Rights April 9 follow-up: 36 Rohingya survivors (from May 2025 navy deportation) in Thailand with no accountability mechanism from India — the non-accountability for a prior floor-conduct event is itself an ACC signal. India at 32.4 Developing — compound negative evidence across EQU, ACC, PRO, SYS dimensions. Assess for score change.

prioritizedEntities.name: Senegal
prioritizedEntities.trigger: 25.6% drop in HIV treatment center attendance in one month due to anti-LGBTQ law chilling effects; 70+ arrested under Article 319 since February; at least 24 charged with 'voluntary HIV transmission' including people on treatment with undetectable viral loads; first 6-year conviction (April 10); HIV Justice Network tracking ongoing detentions.
prioritizedEntities.notes: Last assessed May 9 (band-crossing assessment). The 25.6% HIV treatment attendance drop in one month is a new quantified harm metric not available at time of May 9 assessment — this is measurable, documented, health-harm evidence. 24 charged with HIV transmission including people with undetectable viral loads is a medical-science-denial enforcement pattern (a distinct harm category). 70+ total detained under Article 319 since February. The compound effect of: law (doubled penalties) + enforcement (70+ detained) + healthcare harm (25.6% treatment drop) + criminalization of HIV (24 charged) represents a four-vector harm expansion since last assessment. Composite at 37.5 Developing — potential further downward movement.

prioritizedEntities.name: Ukraine
prioritizedEntities.trigger: May 14 Russia large-scale strike: Ukraine documented civilian infrastructure targeting; Ukrainian counterstrikes targeting Russian command posts in Donetsk, Luhansk, Zaporizhzhia — assessed as defensive; Ukraine advanced in Slovyansk and Huliaipole directions; post-ceasefire conduct record continues to compare favorably to Russia.
prioritizedEntities.notes: Carry-forward from May 13. The May 14 Russian mass strike demonstrates asymmetric conduct: Ukraine sustaining defensive posture, Russia conducting large-scale civilian-area attacks. This strengthens the post-ceasefire conduct-record credit for Ukraine. No evidence of Ukrainian-attributed large-scale civilian targeting in the 24h window. The +3.1 carry-forward credit from May 11 remains the active dimensional item. Assess whether post-ceasefire defensive pattern is sufficient to confirm or expand that credit.

prioritizedEntities.name: UnitedHealth Group
prioritizedEntities.trigger: May 13: Optum Rx disclosed a new transparent fee-based pricing model for PBM services, described as a direct response to pharmacy benefit manager reform legislation — the first proactive transparency reform announcement from UnitedHealth in the current investigation window.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: +1.0 to +2.0 (Optum Rx transparency) offset against ongoing DOJ/AI-denial evidence
prioritizedEntities.notes: Carry-forward from May 12 flag-for-review. The Optum Rx transparent pricing model (May 13) is a new INT-dimension positive signal since the last assessment. Must be weighed against: DOJ probe extended to Optum Rx and physician reimbursement (ongoing), court-ordered AI algorithm disclosure (transparency imposed vs. voluntary), congressional breakup legislation. The transparency reform is reactive/legislative-pressure-driven, not proactively initiated — this limits its ACC weight. Stock recovery (+47% from March lows) is not compassion-relevant. Net delta is likely near-zero or slight positive. Composite at 16.9 Critical — no band-crossing risk.

prioritizedEntities.name: Democratic Republic of Congo
prioritizedEntities.trigger: Amnesty International published May 5 report 'I'd Never Seen So Many Bodies' documenting ADF war crimes and crimes against humanity in eastern DRC: civilian massacres, abductions, forced labor, child recruitment, sexual violence including forced marriage and forced pregnancy; 26.5M people facing acute hunger; OCHA: funding gap forcing strictly prioritized response in 2026.
prioritizedEntities.notes: DRC not confirmed assessed recently — check rotation-state. The Amnesty May 5 report is a major primary-source document covering ADF systematic conduct including sexual violence as a weapon, child soldiers, ransom abduction, and civilian massacres. This is a multi-dimensional assessment event: EMP (26.5M food insecure, 9M displaced by end-2026), ACC (government crackdown on opposition/media continues), SYS (funding gap forcing triage response), PRO (war crimes and crimes against humanity documented by Amnesty). DRC at 7.5 Critical (if previously assessed) — assess for score change. If never assessed: first baseline with heavy adverse evidence.

prioritizedEntities.name: Nigeria
prioritizedEntities.trigger: May 2026: Nigeria has world's highest projected number of acutely food-insecure people — nearly 35 million citizens; 5.8 million facing acute food insecurity during May-October lean season (up from 4.6M prior year); sustained Boko Haram/ISWAP violence in northeast; The New Humanitarian April 27 analysis: 'state dysfunction' preventing adequate humanitarian response.
prioritizedEntities.notes: Rotation carry-forward from May 13 (rank 16 in rotation backfill). The '35 million acutely food insecure — world's highest' designation in May 2026 is a new comparative scale finding. Combined with 5.8M in acute food insecurity during lean season (26% increase year-over-year) — this is a measurable EMP deterioration. State dysfunction analysis (The New Humanitarian, April 27) is a new SYS-dimension primary source. At 18.4 Critical — this is a high-priority rotation assessment. New evidence moves it from rotation slot to score-change candidate.

prioritizedEntities.name: Hungary
prioritizedEntities.trigger: EU Commission dispatched senior official team to Budapest May 13 to assess pathway for unlocking 10.4B EUR in frozen funds; Magyar targeting end-of-May rule of law reform implementation; Brussels visit pencilled for ~May 25; 6.5B EUR in grants + 3.9B EUR in cheap loans dependent on reform delivery.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: watch — no concrete action yet, intent phase; re-assess June 12
prioritizedEntities.notes: Active carry-forward per instruction. EU Commission team dispatch (May 13) is the most concrete institutional signal to date: the EC is actively engaging — this is a procedural milestone that confirms the reform pathway is being institutionally processed. Magyar's end-of-May deadline is the key near-term ACT trigger. No legislative actions confirmed. Band-crossing (37.5 → 41.4) remains pending. Per carry-forward protocol: note this as evidence of process advancement, do not trigger full reassessment until the May 25 Brussels meeting or concrete legislative action.

prioritizedEntities.name: Pakistan
prioritizedEntities.trigger: ECHO Daily Flash May 13 (ReliefWeb): severe weather and flash floods confirmed active across multiple provinces; PDMA active response; weather system continues through May 17; most active period May 13-15; no mass-casualty event confirmed in scan window.
prioritizedEntities.estimatedDelta: watch — EMP dimension pending weather outcome; band crossing already applied
prioritizedEntities.notes: Monsoon watch day 2 of 2 — watch expires today. PDMA/ECHO confirmed active weather event but no mass-casualty flooding documented in 24h window as of scan time. If NDMA situation reports (ndma.gov.pk/sitrepm) show significant casualties or displacement from the May 13-17 system, EMP dimension may generate further downward pressure on a Critical-band entity (composite 20.3). Monitor through May 17 — if no mass-casualty event by May 17, close the watch. The pre-existing Indus Waters Treaty suspension and HRCP civil space documentation remain the primary pending assessment items.

prioritizedEntities.name: South Sudan
prioritizedEntities.trigger: UN Emergency Relief Coordinator Fletcher warned Security Council of risk of 'full-scale famine and collapse'; 10 million people needing humanitarian assistance; 267,000+ displaced in Jonglei state alone in 2026; US aid cuts forcing agency scale-backs; Fangak hospital bombardment (May 3: 7 killed, 20 injured) pending documentation.
prioritizedEntities.notes: Floor entity. Last assessed May 5. HRW May 6 'civilians starving in conflict areas' report is new primary source since assessment. UN aid chief 'full-scale famine and collapse' warning (April 18 UNSC) is a new accountability signal. US aid cut impact is a new EMP-dimension systemic contributor. Fangak hospital bombardment (May 3) remains pending documentation. No score change from 0.0 floor — document as: 'famine-risk escalation' + 'hospital conduct event' + 'aid-withdrawal-induced EMP compounding'.

rotationBackfill.name: Tesla
rotationBackfill.trigger: May 5 NHTSA recall: 218,868 vehicles for rearview camera failure (up to 11 seconds blackout when shifting to reverse); Cybertruck wheel stud separation risk recall also active; H-1B substitution class action proceeding; WARN Act class action proceeding.

The May 5 rearview camera recall (218,868 vehicles) was remediated by OTA update before NHTSA filing — the pre-filing fix is a weak positive process signal. The Cybertruck wheel stud risk is a more severe safety concern. NHTSA-DOGE conflict-of-interest (Musk controls both) remains the most material governance dimension. Never assessed.

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — xAI Prompt Manipulation (Same-Day Event)
sectorAlerts.alert: xAI disclosed a May 14 3:15 AM PST unauthorized modification to Grok's system prompt directing politically biased responses. This is the third distinct Grok governance failure in 2026 (CSAM/NCII January, Musk-protection prompt manipulation February, political-bias modification May 14). xAI announced Grok system prompts will be published on GitHub — a transparency pledge under pressure. This is a same-day assessment trigger for xAI, which has not yet received a baseline assessment.

sectorAlerts.sector: AI Labs — Musk v. Altman Closing Arguments
sectorAlerts.alert: Closing arguments begin May 14-15 in Oakland. Jury to deliberate; Judge Gonzalez Rogers ruling expected mid-May. Trial implications: if charitable trust breach found (Musk's core claim), this is an ACC/SYS scoring event for OpenAI and Microsoft (hold entities). Monitor for verdict — OpenAI hold expires May 21, Microsoft hold expires May 15. Pre-stage both for immediate post-verdict assessment.

sectorAlerts.alert: May 14 active conflict events: Russia large-scale drone/missile attack on Ukraine (1 killed, 36 injured, 1,560+ drones); Myanmar multiple airstrikes (Bilin/Chin — 6 killed, 6 injured including child); Sudan drone strikes continue (Kornoi water well, May 12; 26 killed in Kordofan strikes May 8); South Sudan full-scale famine risk warning (UNSC). India: Transgender Amendment Bill signed (March 2026), removes self-identification right; online censorship expansion rules (April 2026); May 9 arrest for Instagram post. Senegal: 25.6% HIV treatment drop in one month; 70+ detained; 24 charged with HIV transmission including undetectable cases. Both countries show measurable, quantified harm expansion since last assessment. Neither is a floor entity — both are score-change candidates.

This is a high-quality primary-source assessment trigger for DRC, which should be checked in rotation-state for last assessment date.
